exampleJapanese: 隆は厚い本を一気に読み終えた
exampleEnglish: Takashi finished reading the thick book in one go.

exampleJapanese: トレーナーは選手に、運動後に水を浴びるように指導した
exampleEnglish: The trainer instructed the athletes to shower after exercising.

exampleJapanese: 友達の誕生日パーティーで、鈴木さんはたくさんの飴を用意した
exampleEnglish: At a friend’s birthday party, Mr. Suzuki prepared a lot of candy.

exampleJapanese: 「いくつになっても夢を追い続ける姿は美しい」と祖父がよく言っていた
exampleEnglish: “Chasing your dreams no matter how old you get is a beautiful thing,” my grandfather used to say.

exampleJapanese: 星が生まれる瞬間を想像すると、自分の存在がいかに小さいかを思い知らされる
exampleEnglish: When I imagine the moment stars are born, I am reminded of how small my own existence is.

exampleJapanese: 山本さんは公園で遊んでいる元気な男の子を見かけました
exampleEnglish: Mr. Yamamoto noticed a lively boy playing in the park.

meanings: To memorise (Learning and storing information in your memory)
To feel (Physical or emotional sensation in formal, written expressions)
exampleJapanese: 医者は徐々に痛みを覚えた
exampleEnglish: The doctor gradually began to feel pain.

exampleJapanese: 近藤さんは道に迷ってしまい、おまわりさんに助けを求めました
exampleEnglish: Mr. Kondo got lost and asked a police officer for help.

exampleJapanese: その女の子は公園で大きな声で歌いながら、自由に踊っていた
exampleEnglish: The girl was freely dancing while singing loudly in the park.

meanings: Household (Focuses on the family unit / domestic life, not the building)
exampleJapanese: 田中和夫は家庭菜園で新鮮な野菜を育てて毎朝家族に料理を振る舞う
exampleEnglish: Kazuo Tanaka grows fresh vegetables in his home garden and cooks for his family every morning.

exampleJapanese: 彼女は静岡県のお店で美しい花瓶を買った
exampleEnglish: She bought a beautiful vase at a shop in Shizuoka Prefecture.

exampleJapanese: 静かな喫茶店で、一人の老人が冷めたコーヒーを前に何かを思い出しているようだった
exampleEnglish: In a quiet café, an old man sat with a cold cup of coffee in front of him, as if recalling something.

exampleJapanese: 切手を集める趣味が、彼女にとって世界中の文化と触れ合う小さな窓になっている
exampleEnglish: Her hobby of collecting stamps has become a small window for her to connect with cultures around the world.

exampleJapanese: 沙織は天気が曇るのを見て傘を持っていくことにした
exampleEnglish: Seeing that the weather was getting cloudy, Saori decided to take an umbrella.

exampleJapanese: 深夜の公園で警官に声をかけられ、少し緊張しながらも事情を説明した
exampleEnglish: In the park late at night, he was approached by a police officer and, though slightly nervous, explained the situation

exampleJapanese: 看護師は「この薬は結構効きますよ」と説明した
exampleEnglish: The nurse explained, “This medicine works quite well.”

meanings: Entrance (Genkan- Traditional Japanese entryway for house)
exampleJapanese: 玄関に飾られた季節の花が、家に入るたびにほっとする気持ちを与えてくれる
exampleEnglish: The seasonal flowers displayed at the entrance give a sense of relief every time you come home.

exampleJapanese: 夜の交差点で、信号待ちの間に聞こえる街のざわめきがなぜか心に響いた
exampleEnglish: At a crossroads at night, the murmur of the city I heard while waiting for the light somehow resonated in my heart.

exampleJapanese: 彼は道を尋ねるために交番に立ち寄りました
exampleEnglish: He stopped by the police box to ask for directions.

exampleJapanese: 鈴木さんは皆の先に立って、リーダーシップを発揮しました
exampleEnglish: Mr. Suzuki took the lead and demonstrated leadership.

exampleJapanese: 画家は光が差す場所に絵を描いていた
exampleEnglish: The painter was working on his painting in a spot where the light was shining through.

exampleJapanese: 涼介は料理中に塩を少し入れすぎてしまった
exampleEnglish: Ryosuke accidentally added a little too much salt while cooking.

meanings: However (Formal and neutral. Common in writing, speeches and logical arguments. Stronger than けれども)
exampleJapanese: リーダーの提案は魅力的だったが、しかし多くの人が反対した
exampleEnglish: The leader’s proposal was appealing, but many people opposed it.

exampleJapanese: 斉藤裕樹さんは、自動車メーカーでエンジンの開発に携わっています
exampleEnglish: Yuki Saito is involved in engine development at an automobile manufacturer.

meanings: Dictionary (Fairly archaic term that originally referred to a reference book used to look up words or characters)
exampleJapanese: 美咲は難しい言葉を調べるために古い字引を使った
exampleEnglish: Misaki used an old dictionary to look up difficult words

exampleJapanese: 彼女は勝負の直前にスカーフをきつく締めて、小さく深呼吸をした
exampleEnglish: Just before the match, she tightened her scarf and took a small deep breath

exampleJapanese: 雄大は丈夫な靴を選んで山登りに備えた
exampleEnglish: Yūdai chose sturdy shoes in preparation for mountain climbing.

exampleJapanese: 瑠奈は深く息を吸って、落ち着きを取り戻した
exampleEnglish: Runa took a deep breath and regained her composure

exampleJapanese: 彼は新しいズボンを買って試着したが、少し大きすぎた
exampleEnglish: He bought a new pair of trousers and tried them on, but they were a bit too big.

meanings: To live (Refers to where someone resides physically)
exampleJapanese: 彼が山奥に住む理由は、静けさの中で自分の本当の声を聞きたいからだそうだ
exampleEnglish: Apparently, the reason he lives deep in the mountains is that he wants to hear his true inner voice in the silence.

exampleJapanese: 彼女は背が高く、バスケットボールチームのキャプテンを務めている
exampleEnglish: She is tall and serves as the captain of the basketball team.

exampleJapanese: 福島県の学校で、生徒たちが熱心に授業に参加していた
exampleEnglish: At a school in Fukushima Prefecture, the students were actively engaged in their lessons.

exampleJapanese: この石鹸は天然成分で作られており、肌に優しいと評判だ
exampleEnglish: This soap is made from natural ingredients and is reputed to be gentle on the skin.

exampleJapanese: 彼は面接のために背広を着て会社へ行った
exampleEnglish: He wore a suit and went to the company for an interview.

exampleJapanese: 高井さんは週末にまとめて洗濯をして、全部きれいにしました
exampleEnglish: Mr. Takai did all the laundry over the weekend and got everything clean.

exampleJapanese: 美咲は週末に部屋の大掃除をした
exampleEnglish: Misaki did a deep clean of the room at the weekend

exampleJapanese: 莉奈は友達の側に座って、一緒に昼食を食べた
exampleEnglish: Rina sat next to her friend and they ate lunch together.

exampleJapanese: 長崎県の海岸で、彼は青い空を見上げて深呼吸した
exampleEnglish: On the coastline of Nagasaki Prefecture, he looked up at the blue sky and took a deep breath.

exampleJapanese: 裕太は宿題を終えて、それから友達と遊びに行きました
exampleEnglish: Yuta finished his homework and then went out to play with his friends.

exampleJapanese: 健一は海外旅行中に大使館に助けを求めた
exampleEnglish: Kenichi sought help from the embassy while traveling abroad.

exampleJapanese: 鈴木涼子は台所で新しいレシピを試し家族に手作りのケーキを振る舞った
exampleEnglish: Ryoko Suzuki tried a new recipe in the kitchen and served her family a homemade cake.

exampleJapanese: このテーブルは縦に長いデザインです
exampleEnglish: This table has a vertically long design.

exampleJapanese: 誰かが静かな図書館の片隅で、ずっと探していた本を見つけたような微笑みを浮かべていた
exampleEnglish: In a quiet corner of the library, someone was smiling as if they had found a book they had been searching for all along.

meanings: Gradually (Very common in spoken Japanese and often carries a slightly subjective or emotional sense)
exampleJapanese: だんだんと空が赤く染まり、夜の静けさが街全体を包み込んでいった
exampleEnglish: The sky gradually turned red, and the quiet of the night slowly enveloped the entire city.

exampleJapanese: お気に入りの茶碗で飲むお茶は、どんな高級なお菓子よりも心を満たしてくれる
exampleEnglish: Tea drunk from my favorite teacup satisfies my heart more than any fancy sweets.

exampleJapanese: 玲奈は、大手の会社に勤めている
exampleEnglish: Reina works for a large company.

exampleJapanese: 健一は雨が降る前に戸を閉めた
exampleEnglish: Kenichi closed the door before the rain started.

exampleJapanese: お電話ありがとうございますが、どなた様ですか
exampleEnglish: Thank you for calling, but may I ask who this is?

exampleJapanese: 鳥が夕焼けの空を飛ぶ姿は、まるで燃える大地から逃げる影のようだった
exampleEnglish: The sight of a bird flying across the sunset sky looked like a shadow fleeing from a burning earth

exampleJapanese: 公園で色とりどりの鳥が飛んでいるのを見た
exampleEnglish: I saw colorful birds flying in the park.

meanings: Verb to describe the act of an animal making its natural sound
exampleJapanese: 犬が庭で元気に鳴いて、遊んでいる子供たちを呼んでいる
exampleEnglish: The dog is barking energetically in the yard, calling out to the children playing nearby.

exampleJapanese: 真一は小説や詩等の文学に興味を持っている
exampleEnglish: Shinichi is interested in literature, such as novels and poetry.

notes: Intransitive verb describing somethihng that lines up by itself- no one directly acting on the subject to do it
exampleJapanese: お客さんたちは新しいレストランの前に並びました
exampleEnglish: The customers lined up in front of the new restaurant.

exampleJapanese: デザイナーは商品を綺麗に並べました
exampleEnglish: The designer neatly arranged the products.

exampleJapanese: 夏の終わり、海辺の砂に触れると、温い感触が少し切ない気持ちを呼び起こす
exampleEnglish: At the end of summer, when you touch the sand by the sea, its lingering warmth brings about a faint sense of melancholy.

exampleJapanese: 佐々木さんはテーブルに置いてあった灰皿を片付けた
exampleEnglish: Mr. Sasaki put away the ashtray that had been on the table.

exampleJapanese: 佳奈は、友達に送るための葉書を選んでいた
exampleEnglish: Kana was picking out a postcard to send to a friend.

exampleJapanese: 配達員の涼太さんは大きな箱を玄関に置きました
exampleEnglish: The delivery person, Ryota, placed the large box at the doorstep.

exampleJapanese: 鈴木さんは美しい川に架かる橋を渡りながら、景色を楽しんだ
exampleEnglish: While crossing a bridge over a beautiful river, Mr. Suzuki enjoyed the view.

exampleJapanese: 拓海は、寒さで鼻が赤くなっていた
exampleEnglish: Takumi’s nose had turned red from the cold.

exampleJapanese: 松本さんは車の故障に気づいて、すぐにハンドブレーキを引いた
exampleEnglish: Mr. Matsumoto noticed the car malfunctioning and immediately pulled the handbrake.

meanings: Low (height, tone, rank, degree, cost, etc...)
exampleJapanese: 佐々木美優は声が低いと言われることが多いがその落ち着いたトーンで多くの人を安心させている
exampleEnglish: Miyu Sasaki is often said to have a low voice, but her calm tone reassures many people.

exampleJapanese: 昼になると、街は活気にあふれ、人々の話し声や車の音が響き渡る
exampleEnglish: As noon comes, the town fills with energy, and the sounds of people talking and cars echo throughout.

exampleJapanese: 彼は手紙を封筒に入れて、郵便局に持って行った
exampleEnglish: He put the letter in an envelope and took it to the post office.

exampleJapanese: 海翔は強い風が吹く中、海辺を歩いていた
exampleEnglish: Kaito was walking along the seaside in the strong wind.

exampleJapanese: 彼は太い柱の陰に隠れながら、誰にも気づかれないように息を潜めていた
exampleEnglish: While hiding behind a thick pillar, he held his breath so that no one would notice him.

exampleJapanese: 田中さんは感動的な文章を書き、多くの読者の心を動かした
exampleEnglish: Mr. Tanaka wrote a moving piece that touched the hearts of many readers.

exampleJapanese: 公園の辺りにはたくさんの花が咲いていて、散歩するのが楽しいです
exampleEnglish: There are many flowers blooming around the park, and it’s enjoyable to take a walk there.

exampleJapanese: 彼は他の人と違った視点で問題を解決しようとした
exampleEnglish: He tried to solve the problem from a different perspective than others

exampleJapanese: 直子は細いペンで精密なイラストを描きました
exampleEnglish: Naoko drew a detailed illustration with a fine pen.

exampleJapanese: 悠斗は本当の気持ちを友達に打ち明けることにした
exampleEnglish: Yuto decided to share his true feelings with his friend.

exampleJapanese: 陽介はまずい料理でも笑顔で全部食べる優しい性格だった
exampleEnglish: Yosuke had a kind personality, always smiling and finishing even bad-tasting food

exampleJapanese: 春奈は公園で見つけた丸い石を手のひらで転がして遊んでいる
exampleEnglish: Haruna is playing by rolling a round stone she found in the park around in the palm of her hand.

exampleJapanese: 彩花は大切な手紙を書くために万年筆を使った
exampleEnglish: Ayaka used a fountain pen to write an important letter.

exampleJapanese: 菜々美は向こうの山の景色が美しいと感じた
exampleEnglish: Nanami found the view of the distant mountains beautiful.

exampleJapanese: 村の近くで新しい橋が完成し、地元の村長である田中義雄さんが式典で挨拶をしました
exampleEnglish: A new bridge was completed near the village, and the local mayor, Yoshio Tanaka, gave a speech at the ceremony.

exampleJapanese: 佐々木さんは近所の八百屋で新鮮な野菜を買い物しています
exampleEnglish: Sasaki is shopping for fresh vegetables at a local greengrocer.

exampleJapanese: 夕方になると、町の公園には多くの人々が集まり、賑やかな雰囲気に包まれます
exampleEnglish: In the evening, many people gather in the town park, and it becomes filled with a lively atmosphere.

meanings: Dinner (Fairly neutral or old-fashioned. Less childlike)
exampleJapanese: 佐藤さんは仕事から帰った後、友達と一緒に夕飯を作ることにしました
exampleEnglish: After Sato came home from work, he decided to cook dinner with his friend.

exampleJapanese: 昨夜、拓海は遅くまで勉強していた
exampleEnglish: Last night, Takumi was studying until late.

exampleJapanese: 彼女は新しい洋服を買うためにショッピングモールに行きました
exampleEnglish: She went to the shopping mall to buy new clothes.

exampleJapanese: 自転車は長崎県の川の横に止められていた
exampleEnglish: The bicycle was parked beside a river in Nagasaki Prefecture.

exampleJapanese: 真一はより良い結果を目指して努力している
exampleEnglish: Shinichi is working hard to achieve better results.

exampleJapanese: 鈴木さんは体調が弱いと感じて、早めに帰宅した
exampleEnglish: Suzuki felt unwell and went home early.

meanings: Admirable (Often used to praise a person's character, achievement or status. Has a connotation of dignity, respectability and maturity)
exampleJapanese: 彼は立派な業績を残し、地域社会で尊敬される存在となった
exampleEnglish: He achieved remarkable accomplishments and became a respected figure in the local community.

exampleJapanese: 彼の試験の点数は零だったので、再試験を受ける必要がある
exampleEnglish: His exam score was zero, so he needs to retake the test.

exampleJapanese: 田中さんは廊下で友達と立ち話をしていて、授業に遅れそうになりました
exampleEnglish: Mr. Tanaka was chatting with a friend in the hallway and almost ended up late for class.

exampleJapanese: 健太は、忘れ物の書類を田中部長に渡すために急いで会社に戻った
exampleEnglish: Kenta hurried back to the office to hand the forgotten documents to Manager Tanaka.

exampleJapanese: 彼女は提案を聞いて、静かに「いいえ」と答えた
exampleEnglish: She listened to the proposal and quietly answered, “No.”

exampleJapanese: 雅美は熊本の大学で心理学を専攻している
exampleEnglish: Masami is majoring in psychology at a university in Kumamoto.

exampleJapanese: 留学生として初めての冬、雪を見て嬉しくて泣いた日を今でも忘れない
exampleEnglish: I still remember the day I cried with joy when I saw snow for the first time as an international student in my first winter.

meanings: Number (As an identifier, label or ID)
exampleJapanese: 田中健太は受付で自分の番号を呼ばれるのを待っている
exampleEnglish: Kenta Tanaka is waiting at reception for his number to be called

exampleJapanese: 何をするか決める前に、友達と相談しました
exampleEnglish: Before deciding what to do, I talked with my friends.

exampleJapanese: 山本さんは弟にサッカーの練習を手伝ってあげた
exampleEnglish: Yamamoto helped his younger brother with soccer practice.

exampleJapanese: 子供たちは、どれが一番好きな色かを選ぶために色鉛筆を見ている
exampleEnglish: His words were distorted and ended up causing misunderstanding.

exampleJapanese: 旅行者は、どのルートが最も短いかを確認するために地図を見ている
exampleEnglish: The traveler is looking at a map to check which route is the shortest.

exampleJapanese: 旅行者は、あそこに見える山が美しいと感じた
exampleEnglish: The traveler felt that the mountain visible over there was beautiful.

exampleJapanese: 釣り好きな彼は新鮮な魚を市場で買った
exampleEnglish: He, who loves fishing, bought fresh fish at the market.

exampleJapanese: 新鮮な野菜を使ったサラダは健康に良いです
exampleEnglish: A salad made with fresh vegetables is good for your health.

exampleJapanese: 優太は突然の雨に備えて、傘を持って出かけた
exampleEnglish: Yuta took an umbrella with him in case it rained suddenly.

exampleJapanese: 美香は新しい鞄を買って、友達と出かける準備をした
exampleEnglish: Mika bought a new bag and got ready to go out with her friends.

exampleJapanese: 陽介は新しい靴を履いて、久しぶりに友達と遊びに出かけた
exampleEnglish: Yosuke put on new shoes and went out to hang out with his friends for the first time in a while.

exampleJapanese: 警察官の隆一さんは福岡県で落とし物の財布を届けました
exampleEnglish: Police officer Ryuichi turned in a lost wallet in Fukuoka Prefecture.

exampleJapanese: 渡辺さんは晴れた日に自転車で公園を散策するのが好きだ
exampleEnglish: Watanabe likes to explore the park by bike on sunny days

exampleJapanese: 彼女は壁に掛けてある時計を見て、急いで出かける準備をした
exampleEnglish: She looked at the clock hanging on the wall and hurried to get ready to go out.

exampleJapanese: 帽子をかぶった学生が図書館で勉強していた
exampleEnglish: A student wearing a hat was studying in the library.

exampleJapanese: 松本さんは毎月の給料を貯金するために、地元の銀行に定期的に通っている
exampleEnglish: Mr. Matsumoto regularly goes to the local bank to save his monthly salary.

exampleJapanese: 郵便局では荷物の発送や受け取りができます
exampleEnglish: At the post office, you can send and receive packages.

meanings: However much (Used for effort, money, quantity or repeated attempts)
exampleJapanese: 販売員は、商品の値段はいくらかを尋ねられたときに答えた
exampleEnglish: The salesperson answered when asked about the price of the product.

exampleJapanese: 受付のスタッフは、訪問者にどうぞお待ちくださいと優しく声をかけた
exampleEnglish: The receptionist kindly said to the visitor, “Please wait a moment.”

meanings: Dictionary (Most common and general word for dictionary)
exampleJapanese: 彼女は新しい言語を学ぶために、電子辞書を購入しました
exampleEnglish: She bought an electronic dictionary in order to learn a new language.

exampleJapanese: 古い鉛筆を手に取ると、幼い頃に描いた未来の夢がふと頭に浮かんだ
exampleEnglish: When I picked up the old pencil, the dreams of the future I had drawn as a child suddenly came to mind

exampleJapanese: 中村さんはお気に入りの雑誌を毎月購読して、最新の情報をチェックしています
exampleEnglish: Nakamura subscribes to his favorite magazine every month and checks the latest information.

exampleJapanese: 裕太は毎朝ご飯にトーストと卵を食べます
exampleEnglish: Yuta eats toast and eggs for breakfast every morning.

exampleJapanese: 奈美は昼ご飯におにぎりを作って公園で食べました
exampleEnglish: Nami made onigiri for lunch and ate them at the park.

meanings: Dinner (Casual and homey, especially used among family or friends)
exampleJapanese: 今日は家族と一緒に晩ご飯に特別な料理を作る予定だ
exampleEnglish: Today, I plan to make a special meal with my family for dinner.

meanings: House (Refers to the physical house or building)
exampleJapanese: 藤田さんは家をリフォームして、より快適な空間を作ることにしました
exampleEnglish: Mr. Fujita decided to renovate his house to create a more comfortable space.

exampleJapanese: 今晩、彼は友人とディナーに行く予定で、久しぶりに再会することを楽しみにしている
exampleEnglish: He’s planning to have dinner with his friend tonight and is looking forward to seeing him again after a long time.

exampleJapanese: 麻美は土曜日に友達と映画を見に行った
exampleEnglish: Asami went to the cinema with her friend on Saturday.

exampleJapanese: 古びた酒場で赤いランプの下、何を飲むかと聞かれた時、なぜか答えが喉に詰まった
exampleEnglish: In an old bar, under a red lamp, when I was asked what I would drink, for some reason, the answer got stuck in my throat.

meanings: To go to bed (Focuses the act of going to bed or sleeping. Can imply simply lying down)
exampleJapanese: 眠れない夜ほど、「早く寝ろ」と言われるのが腹立たしい
exampleEnglish: The more I can’t sleep at night, the more it annoys me when people tell me to go to bed early.

exampleJapanese: 裕子は「ちょっと待ってください」と言って、話を中断した
exampleEnglish: Yuko said, “Just a moment, please,” and paused the conversation.

exampleJapanese: 彼は時々、友達と一緒に映画を見に行く
exampleEnglish: He sometimes goes to watch movies with his friends.

exampleJapanese: 彼女は友達に「ええ、もちろん行くよ」と答えた
exampleEnglish: She answered her friend, “Yes, of course I’ll go.”

exampleJapanese: 公園で子供たちが犬と遊んでいる姿が見られました
exampleEnglish: I saw children playing with a dog in the park

exampleJapanese: 裕太の猫は、毎朝窓の外を見ています
exampleEnglish: Yuta’s cat looks out the window every morning.

exampleJapanese: 彼女はお気に入りの椅子に座って、小説の続きを書くことに集中していた
exampleEnglish: She sat in her favorite chair, focusing on writing the continuation of her novel.

exampleJapanese: 佳奈は、机の上に教科書を広げて勉強を始めた
exampleEnglish: Kana spread her textbook out on the desk and started studying.

exampleJapanese: 佳恵は、家族の写真をアルバムに整理するのが好きだ
exampleEnglish: Kae likes organizing family photos in albums.

exampleJapanese: 彼は長野の病院で手術を受けることになり、不安と期待が入り混じっていた
exampleEnglish: He was scheduled to undergo surgery at a hospital in Nagano, feeling a mix of anxiety and anticipation.

exampleJapanese: 月曜日は新しい週の始まりで、多くの人が仕事や学校に戻る日だ
exampleEnglish: Monday is the beginning of a new week, a day when many people return to work or school.

exampleJapanese: 火曜日だけ、駅前の時計が午前2時で止まることに誰も触れようとしなかった
exampleEnglish: Only on Tuesdays, no one tried to mention that the clock in front of the station stopped at 2 a.m.

exampleJapanese: 悠斗は木曜日に友達と映画を観に行く予定だ
exampleEnglish: Yuto is planning to go watch a movie with his friends on Thursday.

exampleJapanese: 京都府の古い神社で、偶然昔の友人に会った
exampleEnglish: I ran into an old friend by chance at an ancient shrine in Kyoto Prefecture.

exampleJapanese: 山口舞子は結婚式の写真を撮るために最新のカメラを購入した
exampleEnglish: Maiko Yamaguchi bought the latest camera to take photos at the wedding.

exampleJapanese: 友達を待つ間、彼は本を読んでいた
exampleEnglish: While waiting for his friend, he was reading a book.

meanings: How Come (Neutral to slightly emotional. Very common in daily speech and can show curiosity or frustration)
exampleJapanese: 花子はどうして遅れたのかをみんなに説明した
exampleEnglish: Hanako explained to everyone why she was late.

meanings: Back (Based on position relative to front)
exampleJapanese: 彼は誰かが後ろに立っている気配を感じた
exampleEnglish: He felt someone standing behind him.

exampleJapanese: 一月の澄んだ朝、冷たい空気の中で初日の出がゆっくりと山の稜線から顔を出した
exampleEnglish: On a clear January morning, the first sunrise of the year slowly emerged over the mountain ridge in the cold air.

exampleJapanese: 明後日、彼女は友達と旅行に出かけます
exampleEnglish: The day after tomorrow, she will go on a trip with her friends.

reading: おととし | いっさくねん
exampleJapanese: 一昨年、警官はこの街で大きな事件を解決した
exampleEnglish: The year before last, a police officer solved a major case in this town.

exampleJapanese: 健太は再来年、大学を卒業する予定だ
exampleEnglish: Kenta is scheduled to graduate from university the year after next.

exampleJapanese: 彼女は籠いっぱいの果物を眺めながら、甘い香りの中に子供の頃の夏の記憶を見つけた気がした
exampleEnglish: As she gazed at the basket full of fruit, she felt as though she had found memories of the summers of her childhood within the sweet fragrance.

exampleJapanese: 家族と一緒に海外旅行に行った
exampleEnglish: I went on an overseas trip with my family.

exampleJapanese: ダイバーは海の中で美しい珊瑚礁を探検することに夢中になっている
exampleEnglish: The diver is absorbed in exploring the beautiful coral reefs in the sea.

exampleJapanese: 宿題を終わらせた後の解放感は、何にも代えがたいものがある
exampleEnglish: The sense of relief after finishing homework is something that can’t be replaced by anything else.

exampleJapanese: 彼は自分の部屋をきれいに整頓しました
exampleEnglish: He neatly organized his room.

meanings: Busy (Having many tasks or not enough time)
exampleJapanese: 田中さんは仕事が忙しくて、なかなか休みが取れない
exampleEnglish: Mr. Tanaka is so busy with work that he can hardly take any time off.

meanings: Boring (Focuses on the object being boring/trivial rather than your mental state)
exampleJapanese: 佳奈はつまらない映画を見ている間に、眠くなりました
exampleEnglish: Kana became sleepy while watching a boring movie.

exampleJapanese: 安い時計だったけれど、あの人がくれたものだからずっと捨てられずにいる
exampleEnglish: It was a cheap watch, but because it was something that person gave me, I still haven’t been able to throw it away.

exampleJapanese: 町は祭りのためににぎやかになっています
exampleEnglish: The town is becoming lively because of the festival.

exampleJapanese: 暇なときには縁側に座って、風鈴の音を聞きながら冷たい麦茶を飲むのが一番の贅沢だと思う
exampleEnglish: When I have free time, I think the greatest luxury is sitting on the veranda, drinking cold barley tea while listening to the sound of the wind chimes.

exampleJapanese: 小川さんは夏休みに海で友達と一緒に泳ぐ計画を立てています
exampleEnglish: Mr. Ogawa is making plans to swim in the sea with his friends during summer vacation.

exampleJapanese: 彼女は美しい漢字を書くたびに、まるで一枚の芸術作品を仕上げるようだった
exampleEnglish: Every time she wrote a beautiful kanji character, it was as if she were completing a work of art.

exampleJapanese: 彼女は駅で荷物を持って、友人を探していた
exampleEnglish: She was carrying her luggage at the station and looking for her friend.

meanings: Train (Refers specifically to trains powered by electricity)
exampleJapanese: 雅也は毎朝、電車で通勤している
exampleEnglish: Masaya commutes to work by train every morning.

meanings: Difficult (Often describes something as a requiring a lot of effort, causing stress or being emotionally or physically taxing)
exampleJapanese: 花子は北海道の冬の寒さに慣れるのが大変だった
exampleEnglish: Hanako had a hard time getting used to the winter cold in Hokkaido.

exampleJapanese: 借りた本を返すとき、彼はそのページに挟まれた小さなメモを見つけ、胸がざわついた
exampleEnglish: As he was returning the borrowed book, he found a small note tucked between the pages, and his heart grew uneasy.

exampleJapanese: 編集者は誤った情報を消すために、記事を修正した
exampleEnglish: The editor corrected the article to remove false information.

exampleJapanese: 古びた公園のベンチに座ると、秋の枯葉が風とともに静かに踊り始めた
exampleEnglish: When I sat on the old park bench, the autumn dead leaves quietly began to dance with the wind.

exampleJapanese: 彼は長い間座っていたが、友人が歌い始めると立ち上がって拍手を送った
exampleEnglish: He had been sitting for a long time, but when his friend started singing, he stood up and applauded.

exampleJapanese: 玲奈は図書館に静かに入るところです
exampleEnglish: Reina is about to quietly enter the library.

meanings: To hold (Focuses on the act of physically or metaphorically having or holding something. Doesn't imply movement or transportation)
exampleJapanese: 彼は大切な書類を手に持って会議に向かいました
exampleEnglish: He headed to the meeting carrying the important documents in his hand.

exampleJapanese: 彼女は図書館の本を返すためにカウンターの前で本を閉めました
exampleEnglish: She closed the book in front of the counter in order to return the library book.

exampleJapanese: 彼女は茶道の美しさを教えるたびに、自分自身もその奥深さに魅了されるのだと気づいた
exampleEnglish: Each time she taught the beauty of the tea ceremony, she realized that she herself was also captivated by its profound depth.

exampleJapanese: 彼女の笑顔を忘れることでようやく自分を取り戻せると思っていたが、実際には記憶を消そうとするほど、その存在は逆に鮮明に心に焼き付いていった。
exampleEnglish: He thought that by forgetting her smile he would finally be able to regain himself, but in reality, the more he tried to erase the memory, the more vividly it became etched into his heart.

exampleJapanese: 電車を降りた瞬間、冷たい風が顔を撫でて冬の訪れを実感した
exampleEnglish: The moment I got off the train, a cold wind brushed my face, and I truly felt the arrival of winter.

exampleJapanese: 佐々木さんは友人から本を借りた
exampleEnglish: Sasaki borrowed a book from a friend

exampleJapanese: 中村さんは部屋が暗くなったので、電気を点けた
exampleEnglish: Mr. Nakamura turned on the light because the room had become dark.

exampleJapanese: 美咲は母親に電話するために静かな場所を探した
exampleEnglish: Misaki looked for a quiet place to call her mother.

exampleJapanese: 彼はゆっくり歩きながら、周囲の景色を楽しんでいた
exampleEnglish: He was enjoying the surrounding scenery while walking slowly.

exampleJapanese: 佳奈は北の山々に登って、壮大な景色を楽しんだ
exampleEnglish: Kana climbed the northern mountains and enjoyed the magnificent scenery.

exampleJapanese: 西の空が赤く染まり、今日という一日が静かに幕を下ろしていく
exampleEnglish: The western sky turned red, and the day quietly came to an end.

exampleJapanese: 直樹は東の空に昇る美しい朝日を眺めた
exampleEnglish: Naoki gazed at the beautiful morning sun rising in the eastern sky.

exampleJapanese: 消防士の翔太さんは宮崎県の南の地域で救助活動を行いました
exampleEnglish: Firefighter Shota carried out rescue operations in the southern region of Miyazaki Prefecture.

exampleJapanese: 高橋さんの兄は大学で物理学を専攻しています
exampleEnglish: Takahashi’s older brother is majoring in physics at university.

exampleJapanese: 中村さんは姉の影響で、音楽に興味を持つようになった
exampleEnglish: Mr. Nakamura became interested in music due to his sister's influence

exampleJapanese: 妹は古いアルバムを抱えて戻ってきたが、そこに写る笑顔の彼女は今とはまるで別人だった
exampleEnglish: My younger sister came back holding an old photo album, but the smiling girl in those pictures looked like a completely different person from who she is now.

exampleJapanese: 中村さんは兄弟と一緒に週末のキャンプを楽しみました
exampleEnglish: Mr. Nakamura enjoyed a weekend camping trip with his siblings.

meanings: Company (Refers to a specific individual company or place of work)
exampleJapanese: 彼は新しい会社でキャリアを再スタートさせることに決めた
exampleEnglish: He decided to restart his career at a new company.

exampleJapanese: 駅前の小さな食堂では、毎朝常連客たちがコーヒーと共に何気ない会話を楽しんでいる
exampleEnglish: At the small diner in front of the station, the regular customers enjoy casual conversation with their coffee every morning.

exampleJapanese: 彼は眼鏡をかけて、本をじっくりと読み始めた
exampleEnglish: He put on his glasses and began reading the book carefully.

exampleJapanese: 音楽教師は生徒たちに新しい歌を教えて、合唱の練習を始めた
exampleEnglish: The music teacher taught the students a new song and began choir practice

exampleJapanese: 中村さんは新しい車を購入し、ドライブ旅行を計画しています
exampleEnglish: Nakamura bought a new car and is planning a road trip.

exampleJapanese: 作家は長い物語を執筆するために山小屋にこもっています
exampleEnglish: The writer has secluded himself in a mountain cabin in order to write a long story.

exampleJapanese: デザイナーの杏子は、短い納期のプロジェクトを効率的に仕上げた
exampleEnglish: Kyoko, the designer, efficiently completed a project with a short deadline.

exampleJapanese: 彼の車はとても速く走ります
exampleEnglish: His car runs very fast.

exampleJapanese: 彼女の犬はとてもかわいくて、みんなに愛されている
exampleEnglish: Her dog is very cute and loved by everyone.

exampleJapanese: 中村さんは便利なアプリを使って、旅行の計画を簡単に立てた
exampleEnglish: Using a convenient app, Nakamura easily made plans for the trip.

exampleJapanese: 加藤さんは新しい靴を履いて、公園に散歩に行きました
exampleEnglish: Mr. Katō put on new shoes and went for a walk in the park.

exampleJapanese: 彼女は特別な日にはいつもお気に入りのドレスを着る
exampleEnglish: She always wears her favorite dress on special days.

exampleJapanese: 田中さんは一人で映画を見に行くことが好きだ
exampleEnglish: Mr. Tanaka likes going to watch movies alone.

exampleJapanese: 優斗は毎晩、寝る前にしっかり歯を磨いている
exampleEnglish: Yuto brushes his teeth properly every night before going to bed.

exampleJapanese: 彼は耳を澄ませて、遠くの音を聞こうとした
exampleEnglish: He listened carefully, trying to hear the sounds in the distance

exampleJapanese: 教師は生徒に難しい問題を説明するために彼の頭を掻いた
exampleEnglish: The teacher scratched his head while trying to explain the difficult problem to the students.

meanings: Stomach (As in the general belly area not the organ)
exampleJapanese: 山本さんは朝から忙しくて、昼過ぎにやっとお腹が空きました
exampleEnglish: Yamamoto had been busy since the morning, and only in the early afternoon did he finally get hungry.

exampleJapanese: 明日、旅行に行く予定の香織さんは晴れた天気を楽しみにしています
exampleEnglish: Kaori, who is planning to go on a trip tomorrow, is looking forward to sunny weather.

exampleJapanese: 涼太は曇りの日に散歩をするのが好きだ
exampleEnglish: Ryota likes going for walks on cloudy days.

exampleJapanese: 健太は雪が降る中で雪だるまを作ることにした
exampleEnglish: In the falling snow, Kenta decided to make a snowman.
